projects.Title: Volume By Speed
projects.Subtitle: Andriod Mobile App
projects.AdditionalLinkMessage: View on the Google Play Store
projects.Brief: Volume by Speed is an Android Mobile App that will adjust the volume of the device relative to the speed it’s moving at. This technology is often found in newer cars to counteract road noise that takes away from the audio experience. My app makes this technology accessible and can be used in a variety of similar situations (ie. cycling, skiing, running).
projects.Description:
Volume by Speed is an Android Mobile App that will adjust the volume of the device relative to the speed it’s moving at. This technology is often found in newer cars to counteract road noise that takes away from the audio experience. My app makes this technology accessible and can be used in a variety of similar situations (ie. cycling, skiing, running).
This project came early in my career, and because of its success, it inspired me to continue my software career at San Diego State University. This full-stack project involved creating an intuitive frontend using the tools provided by Android Studio, a back end that accessed location services and adjusted the device’s volume from the background, and connected to Googles ad service to create revenue.
I started working on Volume by Speed in March 2021, and after nearly a year of development, I published it to the Google Play Store in January 2022. Since its launch, the app has undergone several updates and iterations to fix bugs, introduce new features, and enhance the user experience. The latest release (version 7, 1.2.2) was rolled out in May 2023, and no additional updates are currently planned. As of October 2024, Volume by Speed holds a 4.2-star rating with over 50 reviews. While the majority of feedback has been positive, highlighting the app’s usefulness, there have been several constructive criticisms pointing out areas for improvement. I’ve also received close to a dozen direct emails from users requesting new features or offering suggestions, which has been both helpful and motivating in refining the app further. User feedback has played a crucial role in driving improvements, and it’s been gratifying to see the app resonate with others.
Volume by Speed was the largest project I had taken on up to that point, resulting in countless ‘learn by doing’ moments. As a solo developer, I relied heavily on online resources, which made the development process slower but deeply educational. This project has been a cornerstone of my journey in software engineering and computer science. It was the first major app I built entirely on my own, from concept to completion. Having a finished product that I, and others, find genuinely useful is incredibly rewarding. This project represents my growth as a developer and serves as a tangible example of my passion for creating meaningful software.

projects.Title: Photo Sort
projects.Subtitle: Python App
projects.Brief: Photosort is a Python script to aid in sorting massive amounts of media.
projects.Description: Photosort is a Python script to aid in sorting massive amounts of media. The script will deconstruct file trees, then reconstruct a file tree, placing files in directories based on their timestamp and location data.

projects.Description:
zerofour.tech acts as a personal portfolio for my projects and career in the tech industry. The website and projects pertaining to the zerofour.tech domain are ongoing projects.
The website is written using React and responds well to mobile and desktop environments.

projects.Title: ALEXHIXSON.COM
projects.Subtitle: React Web App
projects.Brief: A personal website to explore webdev and related technologies
projects.Description:
A personal website to explore webdev and related technologies.
 One of my recent projects with the site is a message board that anyone (anyone with an access key) can write to. By sending an email to the message board email address with either an approved email or access key, a server side script with parse and store your message. Your message will then be displayed on on the front page alongside other messages.
 This project involved configuring email piping and scripting to parse the email. I also have employed several security measures to prevent injection to the database. I really enjoyed setting this up and am happy with how it turned out. Go to alexhixson.com to see product and ask about getting an access code!
[While I do own the domain alexhixson.com, the site is hosted on a subdomain of zerofour.tech for hosting reasons. Visiting alexhixson.com will redirect you to alexhixson.zerofour.tech]

projects.Title: MorseCoding
projects.Subtitle: Course Work Project
projects.Brief: A coding language that could be written using only a single key.
projects.Description:
MorseCoding - for people with only one key
Only Requires one button - Thus it can be extremely useful on smaller or limited hardware.
The language is very lightweight and can be easily installed anywhere
Simple, in that it can be visualized with the following tree 
0’s and 1’s
The language is written in only 0’s and 1’s
This can be done with a traditional keyboard by pressing 0 and 1
Or, with only one key, a program will scan the key every second to determine whether a 0 or 1 should be written. Thus, it can be written with only 1 key. Sample Programs
The words, letters, and spaces are not interpreted, they just help with readability. The program could be written like this. But it is far less clear what it does in this form both these programs generate the same output.

projects.Title: Music Translator
projects.Subtitle: Python App
projects.Brief: Music Translator takes any song by URL, separates vocals from instrumental, translates the lyrics to match the original syllable count and stress patterns, clones the singer's voice, and outputs a finished track in the target language.
projects.Description:
Music is universal but language isn't. Music Translator was built to hear songs in different languages without losing the original singer's voice or feel — and to automate the hardest part: making translated lyrics actually fit the music.
The pipeline runs in three stages. First, yt-dlp, demucs, and Whisper handle audio separation and transcription. Second, a multi-agent loop using uAgents runs Claude Haiku for translation while syllable and stress critic agents score each line, rejecting and revising until the output meets a quality threshold. Third, ElevenLabs voice cloning and time-stretching via pyrubberband fit the translated audio back to the original timing.
Some of the trickier challenges included Claude flagging copyrighted lyrics — fixed with prompt reframing and assistant prefill — syllable matching across languages with different natural densities, and time-stretching artifacts, which were solved with pause-aware stretching that warps only speech, not silence. Whisper cache reuse across songs was also fixed by hashing audio file contents as the cache key.
The result is a fully automated pipeline: URL in, translated MP3 out. The multi-agent critique loop with quantitative metrics produces genuinely singable translations that actually sound like the original singer performing in another language.
Key takeaways: prompt framing matters enormously, multi-agent critique loops with measurable constraints outperform single-model self-evaluation, and audio time-stretching requires preserving silence structure, not just phoneme quality.
What's next: better translation search with beam and sampling over candidates, prosody-aware TTS, broader language support, and a web UI.
